acceptanceCriteria: ProcessIO (or a host variant) writes raw Uint8Array to stdout/stderr with no forced trailing LF
Writes are awaitable and surface write errors (closed-pipe/EPIPE) so a CLI can map them to exit 74
Bounded readStdin/readFile byte readers available (e.g. 16MiB cap)
A flush-before-exit path so large stdout is not truncated (vs process.exit)
immortalizer-cli can migrate onto the host boundary with emittedCli.spec byte-identical
description: cli-kit 1.0.2 ProcessIO is a console/string plane (log/warn/error variadic-unknown that stringify + append a trailing LF, return void; nodeProcessHost = console.log/console.error). This blocks byte-exact CLIs from adopting the host boundary — immortalizer-cli's migration was DEFERRED 2026-07-22 because: (1) canonicalize must emit raw bytes with NO trailing LF; (2) diagnostics are exact 'immortalizer: <code>\n' on stderr; (3) closed-stdout must map to exit 74 by observing the write-callback error — ProcessIO.log returns void and swallows async EPIPE; (4) bounded 16MiB stdin/file byte reads have no home in ProcessIO. Separately, cli-kit-node host.exit()=process.exit(code) can TRUNCATE un-flushed stdout on large payloads (immortalizer uses process.exitCode to drain first). Need either (A) a byte-plane extension to ProcessIO: awaitable writeStdout/writeStderr(Uint8Array) that surface write errors, plus bounded readStdin/readFile byte readers; and a flush-before-exit path; OR (B) a bring-your-own-IO host that lets the app own a non-ProcessIO byte channel while still using the host's exit/signal/onInterrupt boundary. Unblocks immortalizer-cli (byte-pinned emittedCli.spec) and lifehash-indexer's stdout-is-data discipline.
title: ProcessIO byte-plane: awaitable Uint8Array writers + bounded byte readers for byte-exact CLIs
verification: immortalizer-cli emittedCli.spec byte-identical after a future migration; cli-kit unit tests for the byte writers/readers
